findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised equivalent of lines 173-233 contains no two consecutive exchanges that only relay policy/logistics and each retained exchange ends with a new irreversible constraint or choice for Eli; fail if the sequence still reads as three straight administrative briefings.
findings.problem: The back half settles into sequential institutional briefings (PA, registrar, social worker) that mostly transfer logistics. This over-literal reuse of Eli's style-bible stress pattern (logistics first) flattens escalation, so leverage shifts weaken after the crash peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the run from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:221 so at least one info-delivery exchange is compressed and replaced by a conflict beat that forces a harder tradeoff (money vs transport vs relationship contact) in-scene.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if one live Nora-or-June exchange is rendered in-scene and includes all three: (1) Eli's physical impairment in the moment, (2) a direct challenge/boundary from the family member, and (3) no immediate emotional tidy-up; fail if contact remains text-only or summarized.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the emotional apex: after a near-fatal pileup, family confrontation is routed through efficient text snippets, which summarizes consequence instead of rendering live relational pressure. The scene stays safe and controlled where it should be messier.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the contact block anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:249, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:259, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:271, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:281 so at least one real-time call occurs on page with interruption, breath/pain limits, and unresolved accusation or boundary.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least four of those speakers each have one line that is recognizable without dialogue tags (distinct vocabulary/rhythm unique to that role); fail if lines remain easily swappable between characters.
findings.problem: Secondary speakers are under-separated: Trooper Chen, Dario, the nurse, registrar, and social worker share a near-identical clipped competence cadence, making multiple voices interchangeable under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:185, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:221, differentiate idiolect by role-specific diction, syntax length, and interaction style rather than uniform efficient exposition.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each revised counterpart line contains natural spoken texture (at least one contraction or interruption marker) and no uninterrupted policy monologue runs beyond two clauses; fail if dialogue still reads like written discharge copy.
findings.problem: Stiff over-formality drifts into patient-facing speech in multiple spots (long, policy-clean, lightly contracted blocks), which fights the book-wide default of high spoken contraction and pressure-textured rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:221 with contractions, interruptions, and shorter spoken chunks while preserving factual content.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage keeps no more than two 'verb + once' constructions across those four anchors and each retained gesture carries scene-specific emotional or physical context; fail if three or more generic '... once' beats remain.
findings.problem: A recurring gesture template (verb + 'once') appears four times and reads as mechanical prose fallback rather than purposeful rhythmic design.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:87, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:181, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:183, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:195 by replacing at least two 'verb + once' instances with action tied to motive or sensation specific to the moment.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised opening contains at most two past-perfect clauses and a first-read chronology remains unambiguous; fail if past-perfect remains the dominant sentence frame across the first five paragraphs.
findings.problem: The opening stacks past-perfect scaffolding ('had' clauses) across multiple consecutive paragraphs, which softens immediacy before the scene's first active conflict.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the opening block at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:13, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:15, keep one clear prior-time anchor and convert the rest to simple past where chronology still reads cleanly.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2895 conveys shock without any "landed" formula; FAIL if the same abstract-impact template remains.
findings.problem: Same full-book recurring formula metaphor pattern (8-chapter spread) appears here as "The number landed..." impact shorthand. Chapter action: REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2895 so the billing shock is rendered through fresh physical cognition (breath, posture, motor decision) instead of "landed" impact wording.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
